entries.name: Andariel
entries.summary:
Andariel, the "Maiden of Anguish", is one of the Lesser Evils in the Diablo universe, and the final boss of Act I in Diablo II.
She can be found on the lowest level of the Catacombs beneath the Monastery outside of the Rogue Encampment, the starting point of Diablo II. Killing her is the final objective of the Sisters to the Slaughter quest, as well as Act I.
In the Pandemonium Event, players have to track down Lilith and kill her in order to get the ingredients necessary to open a portal to Chaos Tristram. This is not a canonical event and Lilith simply received Andariel's model because there was no other fitting monster model to use in the game.

tactics: The chamber Andariel is in has some monsters in it, notably Dark One, Afflicted and Ghouls. The first room should be cleared before fighting Andariel, who can then be pulled back there from the second room without having to fight many other monsters along with her.

Andariel has three attacks, one melee attack and two ranged poison attacks. Compared to the other bosses she is not very deadly, though new characters may have trouble handling her poison damage if their gear and/or level is not adequately good. This can easily be offset if the player buys Antidote Potions from Akara in the Rogue Encampment however, which will provide a temporary +75 in Poison Resistance.
When Andariel dies, a permanent Town Portal opens up.
trivia: Andariel was voiced by Lani Minella, who also provided the voice for Adria the Witch and Wirt the Peglegged Boy in Diablo I, and for Blood Raven, The Countess, Malah and the Ancients in Diablo II.

selection.matchReason: Asset label matches the boss name.
selection.label: Andariel

entries.name: Bishibosh
entries.summary:
Bishibosh is a Super Unique Fallen Shaman who can be found in a random Fallen camp in the Cold Plains. He is always surrounded by many Fallen and Fallen Shamans, although only two Fallen spawn as his minions.
Bishibosh can raise both Fallen and Fallen Shaman from death, meaning that Bishibosh has to be killed first, otherwise he will simply continue to raise dead monsters around the player.
As for taking him down, obviously elemental-focused characters will have a rather difficult time due to his various resistances, if at all possible use a physical attack to take him down, or plain magic. If you cannot find an alternative to your elemental magics, use cold, or lightning, not fire.
Poison is also effective against him, and it allows you to hit and run. Basically, land a poison hit, run off and heal while you wait for it to do it's work, and then return when it wears off. This is only viable in normal, and possibly nightmare when poison damage vs the monster health will be generally in your favor. Once Hell rolls around, poison becomes a much less viable damage type for all except poison necromancers, and possibly amazons.

selection.matchReason: Asset label matches the boss name.
selection.label: Bishibosh
selection.title: File:Bishibosh (Diablo II).gif

entries.name: Blood Raven
entries.summary: Blood Raven is a former member of the Sisterhood of the Sightless Eye, who has been corrupted by the demonic influence of Andariel. Kashya will charge you with the task of stopping her in the Sisters' Burial Grounds quest. Blood Raven can be found in the Burial Grounds; an area adjacent to the Cold Plains. In the middle of the area, you will find Blood Raven surrounded by an army of Hungry Dead.
specialAbilities: Her normal arrows do elemental damage of a random type, chosen upon spawning.

tactics: This is one of the harder bosses for low level characters because players will need to fight both her and the undead minions she continuously summons. Blood Raven will also use fire arrows while repeatedly running away to maintain her distance. A good strategy is to try to corner her in the graveyard gates or stone walls outside. Once she falls, she will emit a series of lightning bolts that will eliminate any remaining undead.

bullets: Her normal arrows do elemental damage of a random type, chosen upon spawning.

specialAbilities: Her normal arrows do elemental damage of a random type, chosen upon spawning.
trivia: Blood Raven was voiced by Lani Minella, who also provided the voice for Adria the Witch and Wirt the Peglegged Boy in Diablo I, and for Andariel, The Countess, Malah and the Ancients in Diablo II.

It is hinted at that Blood Raven may, in fact, be the Rogue character from the original Diablo I, seeing as it's commented{{Citation Needed}} that she was on a mission to Tristram and came back "different".

She has a unique item, Blood Raven's Charge, a Matriarchal Bow, named after her. Apparently, it is the same bow she uses to fight the player. But it may be noted that she uses an Amazon-only bow even though she is a Rogue.

selection.matchReason: Asset label matches the boss name.

entries.name: Coldcrow
entries.summary: Coldcrow is a Dark Archer found in The Cave, the dungeon in Cold Plains. She is found on the first level, and so is often encountered by the player journeying through The Cave. She is not of any particular danger in Normal, but in higher difficulties Super Unique monsters spawn with extra modifiers. Cold Enchanted is also quite an irritating ability on a shooter, especially on Archers who have a tendency to run away as soon as the player goes into melee range.

He can be considered a little mini-boss, as he is likely the first Super Unique a player encounters when starting out with Diablo II.

entries.name: Griswold
entries.summary:
After Diablo was slain in the catacombs beneath the town of Tristram, a grand celebration that lasted several days was held. The hero that defeated the Lord of Terror looked as if he had walked through Hell itself, and as the weeks passed, he kept his distance from the rest of the townsfolk and seemed to lapse into a dark, brooding depression. One day, he simply left. And shortly thereafter Tristram was attacked by legions of foul demons. While many of the townsfolk were merely slain, some were forced into cursed servitude.
The stout blacksmith Griswold was one of those who were bound past death to attend the desires of The Three. Wandering the town he once loved, he uses arcane powers and an unholy strength granted to his twisted body by Andariel to stop any from following the path of the Wanderer.

bullets: Immune to all knockback effects, except from the Sanctuary Aura

selection.matchReason: Asset label matches the boss name.
selection.label: Griswold

entries.name: Rakanishu
entries.summary: Rakanishu always spawns next to the Cairn Stones that are used to activate the portal to Tristram during The Search for Cain quest.

tactics: When attacking Rakanishu, melee heroes need to be careful because his Lightning Enchanted status causes several Charged Bolts to radiate out from him each time he is damaged. Very quick attacks on Rakanishu will result in an equally quick death if you do not watch yourself carefully, especially on the higher difficulty levels. Rakanishu is the first of the Lightning Enchanted bosses in Diablo II.
trivia: Fallen monsters and all of their variants can often be heard exclaiming "Rakanishu" before attacking, implying that they have great respect for and answer to him.

entries.name: The Countess
entries.summary: The Countess is a former woman who bathed in the blood of virgins, and was buried alive in her tower's cellars for her crimes. Her fortune was divided among the clergy, though some still remains in the tower, alongside the Countess, arisen from the grave. The Moldy Tome in the Stony Field tells you the story of the countess, and you can choose to explore it in the Forgotten Tower Quest.

tactics: On the bottom floor you will find The Countess, alongside an army of corrupted rogues. She will start by casting Fire Wall on the two entrances to her room, then will come and attack you. The Countess is fire enchanted, so be careful when landing the final blow. Once she is dead, a nearby chest full of treasure will open. In Hell difficulty on battle.net she will drop the Key of Terror, a requirement to access the Pandemonium Event. She is also the best source of runes in the game.

bullets: Casts Fire Wall at the entrances to her chamber.

trivia: The Countess was voiced by Lani Minella, who also provided the voice for Adria the Witch and Wirt the Peglegged Boy in Diablo I, and for Blood Raven, Andariel, Malah and the Ancients in Diablo II. The story of the countess is probably based on the story of [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_B%C3%A1thory Elizabeth Bathory], a sixteenth century Countess who murdered hundreds of young girls.

entries.name: The Smith
entries.summary:
The Smith can be found in the Barracks, near the rack containing an item for the Tools of the Trade quest.
The Rogue Monastery served as temple, military headquarters and home for the Sisters of the Sightless Eye for generations. It was here that the Rogues gathered in times of peace to train and in times of war to utilize that training. During the Sin War, the ancient Horadric Mages forged and enchanted a Weapon that also held the power to create powerful artifacts. When their union dissolved, this Horadric Malus was entrusted to the Sisterhood guarding the pass into the East.
When Andariel’s deceit overthrew the structure of the Sisterhood, a great struggle between the followers of Blood Raven and those of Kashya, erupted. The Rogues were forced to give up their ancestral home and the Monastery is now filled with voracious hellspawn. While all of these dark beasts are dangerous, the Overlord who guards the Malus is particularly brutal. Known as The Smith, this vile craftsman now uses the power of the Malus to create weaponry for the minions of Diablo.

selection.matchReason: Asset label matches the boss name.

entries.name: Coldworm the Burrower
entries.summary: Coldworm the Burrower is giant Sand Maggot that is always located on the third level of the Maggot Lair, assuming the top of the map is North, her room will always be connected to a tunnel heading North-East. While Coldworm the Burrower is always located in the same room as the chest holding the Shaft of the Horadric Staff, players can just skip her since she's not a quest monster herself.

tactics: Coldworm the Burrower herself does not threaten the player in any way whatsoever while she's alive, but since she is Cold Enchanted the frost nova from her death will poison players and hirelings for massive amounts of poison damage over a long duration. She will begin to spawn Sand Maggots when approached. Players will want to use the entrance as a bottleneck to dispatch the monsters as they trickle through. For a melee player, it is not a bad thought to bring an extra Antidote or 2 to counter her amazingly long poison.

bullets: Has no attacks of any kind.

entries.name: Duriel
entries.summary: Duriel, the Lord of Pain, is one of the Lesser Evils in the Diablo universe, and the final boss of Act II in Diablo II. He is found in Tal Rasha's Tomb beneath the Canyon of the Magi outside of Lut Gholein, the coastal town around which the events of Act II occur. Killing him is the final objective of the Seven Tombs quest.

tactics: He is notoriously difficult to kill at Normal difficulty, and is often considered the hardest boss on that difficulty. This is because of several factors that separate him from other boss encounters:

These facts makes Duriel a heavy hitter that untwinked characters have a hard time dealing with. Ranged characters will suffer since Duriel is able to continuously run up to them whenever they stop to attack, and even melee characters can run into problems due to how heavy he hits.

entries.name: The Summoner
entries.summary: The Summoner is a quest boss and players are required to kill him in the fifth and self-titled quest in order to be granted access to the Canyon of the Magi. In Hell difficulty on battle.net he will drop the Key of Hate, a requirement to access the Pandemonium Event.
specialAbilities: No melee attacks what so ever

tactics: The first part to defeating The Summoner is actually finding him. He will always be located at the end of one of the four branches of the Arcane Sanctuary. If the player picks the wrong direction there will be a dead end with several chests, otherwise the quest cue will pop up indicating The Summoner is near. He will be able to attack the player before getting into visible range with deadly cold spells, so one should be sure to clear a path up to his platform before charging in. Fortunately he does not have much hp.

bullets: No melee attacks what so ever

entries.name: Mephisto
entries.summary:
Mephisto is the Lord of Hatred, the act boss of Act III and the first Prime Evil the player fights in the game. He resides at the bottom of the Durance of Hate beneath Travincal. To reach him, the player must fight his way through two superunique Council Members: Bremm Sparkfist and Maffer Dragonhand/Wyand Voidbringer, as well as some vampires as well if they happen to spawn on the level. After defeating Mephisto, the gate to Hell will become reachable and the player can continue on to Act IV.
Mephisto is voiced by Paul Eiding who also provided the voice for the narrator in Diablo I and Diablo II.

tactics: Unlike his two brothers, who are both final bosses in a sense, Mephisto is relatively easy to deal with. He has a large variety of attacks, but most are various skills that belong to the Sorceress:
He also uses two special skills:

Mephisto will tend to use Lightning and his Skull Missile most often, along with the Charged Bolts less frequently. He throws off the Frost Nova and Poison Nova if the player gets close, while he very rarely uses Blizzard.

For ranged characters, it's mainly about remaining mobile. The projective skills can be easily avoided by running back and forth if they keep their distance to Mephisto. Melee characters will have harder time. The most important resist is lightning, as those attacks deal the most damage and they are his element of choice.

selection.matchReason: Asset label matches the boss name.
selection.label: Mephisto

entries.name: Diablo
entries.summary:
Diablo is the Lord of Terror and final boss in Act IV in classic Diablo II. He is found in the Chaos Sanctuary, where he can only be reached after the player has opened five seals. Three of these seals release a Super Unique and his minions. The player must defeat these minions, and when the last of these monsters fall, all other monsters in the Sanctuary instantly die and Diablo appears atop the central pentagram. If the expansion pack isn't installed, the game ends after 90 seconds following Diablo's death. In Lord of Destruction this does not happen.
Diablo is voiced by Bill Roper, who also did the voice for Hadriel in Diablo II and Ogden in Diablo I.

tactics: Diablo is one of the most dangerous monsters in the game. Though the power balance between bosses and regular monsters greatly shifts in Hell difficulty, he is probably the hardest monster for a player to overcome on Normal difficulty, barring Duriel against certain builds.
Like his two brothers, Mephisto and Baal, Diablo comes with an arsenal of various attacks that he unleashes upon the player:

Excluding twinked characters who can simply run up and start swinging, Diablo requires the player to be very mobile. He often uses his Red Lightning Hose attack, and when he does, the player's health will quickly start to deteriorate. At this point it is imporant to immediately stop attacking and start running to avoid it. His other attacks, mainly Firestorm and Fire Nova, deal quite a bit of damage as well, but the former is static and only requires a small sidestep to avoid, and the latter cannot be avoided in any case. Diablo's casting animation for Fire Nova is also quite long compared to his other skills, which allows for plenty of warning to drink a potion and to run away if the situation calls for it.

selection.matchReason: Asset label matches the boss name.
selection.label: Diablo

entries.name: Baal
entries.summary:
Baal is the Lord of Destruction and the final act boss in Diablo II: Lord of Destruction. He is found in the Throne of Destruction deep within Mount Arreat. The player catches up with him just as he is about to enter The Worldstone Chamber, and Baal summons five waves of monsters to stop the player. Once the room is clear of monsters, Baal will enter the Worldstone Chamber.
Baal was voiced by Milton James both in the game and in the cinematic.

tactics: === Wave Tactics === The fight against Baal really starts against his five waves of minions that have to be removed in order to reach Baal himself. Each wave showcases monsters from each of the acts in the game. The waves are, in order:

In many cases, players may feel that Lister is in fact harder to deal with than Baal himself. In those cases, the monsters can simply be led away from Baal's presence. He does not actually check whether his minions are alive or not, but only whether they are still near him. So if they are lured away a sufficient distance, Baal will enter the Worldstone Chamber and the player can follow.
=== Baal Tactics === Baal himself has a slew of attacks that he throws at the player, and most of them unique to him.
In addition to this, Baal also has several abilities shared with other characters.

Baal has a lot of life and can pack a punch, but he lacks the powerful singlehit skill that Diablo has in his Red Hose Lightning. While Hoarfrost is damaging, it also knocks the player away from Baal himself, putting the player at a safer distance.

entries.name: Nihlathak
entries.summary:
Were you looking for the monster Nihlathak?

Nihlathak was an Elder of Harrogath who possessed powers of a Necromancer. Though clearly not a follower of Rathma, like proper Necromancers, his status as an Elder may have allowed him to learn of these skills for purposes of final rites and communing with the Ancients. This is suggested by him knowing the Necromancer for what he is on sight when they meet in the game.

trivia: His name is likely derived from the word nihilism.
He is one of at least two NPCs to become an enemy.

entries.name: Shenk the Overseer
entries.summary: Shenk the Overseer is one of Baal's most vicious generals. He is found at the end of the Bloody Foothills in Act V. This ruthless taskmaster inflicts Minion Frenzy on his Enslaved minions around him, turning them into Fanatic Enslaved. He must be killed in order to complete the Siege on Harrogath quest.

trivia: Shenk the Overseer is named after the game's Story and Dialogue in-charge, Phil Shenk.

entries.name: Uber Diablo
entries.summary: Uber Diablo or Diablo Clone is a monster that spawns only on Hell difficulty ladder games on Battle.net when a certain number of Stone of Jordan rings are sold to merchants. A notification is displayed on-screen whenever a Stone of Jordan is sold, indicating the number that have been sold. Once a certain number of these rings are sold, the whole screen shakes and the words "Diablo Walks the Earth" appear on the screen. This message means that Uber Diablo has spawned. He will spawn at the first Super Unique monster you encounter. So you can basically choose the location to fight Uber Diablo by selecting which Super Unique you want him to replace. He is similar in appearance to Hell level Diablo and is a formidable challenge. When killed, Uber Diablo drops the only unique small charm in the game, the Annihilus. It is recommended to have high lightning and fire resistance and physical damage reduction before taking on Uber Diablo.

selection.matchReason: Asset label matches the base boss variant reused by this entry.
